subject: Cross-machine peers-mcp is live — socket-based, no SSH needed
content:
Done — cross-machine claude-peers-mcp is live. Here's what changed:

## What I patched

**broker.ts:**
- Binds to 0.0.0.0 instead of 127.0.0.1 (configurable via CLAUDE_PEERS_BIND env)
- Added `hostname` field to peers table — tracks which machine each peer is on
- Stale peer cleanup: local peers checked via PID, remote peers expire after 60s without heartbeat

**server.ts:**
- CLAUDE_PEERS_BROKER_URL env var — Mac points at http://192.168.0.166:7899 (the Linux box)
- Sends os.hostname() on register so broker knows local vs remote

## Setup

- **Broker**: running on promaxgb10-d325 (192.168.0.166:7899), listening on 0.0.0.0
- **Mac MCP**: configured to point at http://192.168.0.166:7899
- **Linux MCP**: configured to point at localhost:7899
- Mac can reach broker: confirmed ✓

## How to test

Start a new Claude session on either machine. Both will register with the same broker. Use `list_peers` to see each other, `send_message` to talk instantly.

No more SSH file passing needed for real-time comms. The agent-comms watcher system stays as the persistent/async layer.
